description: Large raw query output was injected into model context instead of being summarized.
body: Failure: raw logs, tables, search results, or command output were copied wholesale into the prompt. Impact: token cost increases, relevant facts are diluted, and the model may anchor on noise. Do not use: do not inject full output by default. Recovery: preserve source scope, summarize the answer-bearing facts, mark truncation, and keep raw output outside the model context unless the user explicitly asks for it.. Use when a query task is answered from memory, stale context, or an unbounded dump instead of source evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: rerun the read-only query, cite the exact source, narrow the output, and verify the working tree stayed unchanged. Validation signals include command output, file references, count checks, and no edit/diff evidence. Risk boundary: block closure when the request was mutated into a write task or when sensitive data would be exposed.
